html:
[1412]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 久之 - Indicates a passage of time; often translated as 'after a long time' or 'for a long time'.
  • 倭人寇江南 - Japanese raiding the southern regions of China; '倭人' refers to the Japanese, '寇' means to raid or invade, and '江南' refers to the Yangtze River area.
  • 掠海上 - '掠' means to plunder or raid, and '海上' refers to the coastal areas.
  • 焚其邑 - '焚' means to burn, and '邑' refers to a town or city.
  • 翘儿窜走桐乡 - '翘儿' is the name of a person, '窜走' means to flee or escape, and '桐乡' is a place name.
  • 已而转掠桐乡 - '已而' means 'afterward' or 'later,' and '转掠' means to move on to plunder another area.
  • 城陷 - '城' means city or town, and '陷' means to fall or be captured.
  • 翘儿被虏 - '被虏' means to be captured or taken captive.
  • 诸酋执以见其寨主徐海 - '诸酋' refers to various tribal leaders, '执' means to seize or take, and '见其寨主' means to present to the chief of the camp.
  • 徐海者，故越人，号明山和尚者是也 - '徐海' is the name of a person, '故越人' means he was originally from the Yue region, and '号明山和尚' is his nickname or title.
  • 海初怪其姿态不类民间妇女 - '初' means initially, '怪' means to find strange, '姿态' refers to appearance or demeanor, '不类' means not resembling, and '民间妇女' refers to ordinary women.
  • 讯之，知为翘儿 - '讯之' means to interrogate or question, and '知为翘儿' means to find out that she is Qiao'er.
  • 试之吴，及弹胡琵琶，以侍酒，绝爱幸之，尊为夫人 - '试之吴' means to test her in Wu (a region), '弹胡琵琶' means to play the Huqin (a type of Chinese lute), '以侍酒' means to serve wine, '绝爱幸之' means to greatly love and favor her, and '尊为夫人' means to honor her as a lady.
  • 斥帐中诸姬罗拜，咸呼之为王夫人 - '斥' means to dismiss or send away, '帐中诸姬' refers to the women in the tent, '罗拜' means to bow in a circle, and '咸呼之为王夫人' means they all call her Lady Wang.
  • 翘儿既已用事 - '用事' means to be in power or influence.
  • 凡海一切计画惟翘儿意指使 - '凡海一切计画' means all of Xu Hai's plans, '惟' means only, '翘儿意指使' means according to Qiao'er's intentions.
  • 乃翘儿亦阳昵之，阴实幸其败事，冀一归国以老也 - '阳昵之' means to outwardly show affection, '阴实幸其败事' means secretly hoping for his failure, '冀一归国以老也' means hoping to return to her country to grow old.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 倭人 - Refers to the Japanese, who were known for their raids on the Chinese coast during the Ming and Qing dynasties.
  • 江南 - Refers to the southern regions of China, particularly the Yangtze River area, which was a wealthy and culturally significant region.
  • 桐乡 - A specific place name, likely referring to a town in the Jiangsu or Zhejiang provinces.
  • 徐海 - A historical figure, known as a pirate leader in the late Ming dynasty. He was originally from the Yue region and had the nickname 'Ming Shan Heshang' (明山和尚).
  • 王夫人 - A title given to Qiao'er, indicating her high status in Xu Hai's camp.

Proper Nouns

  • 倭人 - Japanese people.
  • 江南 - Southern regions of China.
  • 桐乡 - A specific place name, likely in Jiangsu or Zhejiang provinces.
  • 徐海 - A historical pirate leader.
  • 明山和尚 - Nickname of Xu Hai.
  • 翘儿 - The name of the protagonist.
  • 王夫人 - A title given to Qiao'er.

[1413]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 会督府遣华老人檄召 - Formal and official act indicating a high-level diplomatic or military engagement.
  • 海怒而缚华老人将斩之 - Strong emotional reaction and severe threat.
  • 翘儿谏曰 - Dialogue and the role of Qiao'er as an advisor.
  • 今日之势在君 - Statement of power and responsibility.
  • 降不降何与来使也 - Emphasizes personal decision-making.
  • 亲解华老人缚而厚与之金劳苦也 - Change of heart and gesture of goodwill.
  • 华老人者，海上人也 - Provides background information about the old man.
  • 翘儿故识之 - Indicates a personal connection.
  • 而华老人亦私觑所谓王夫人者 - Suggests a hidden agenda or interest.
  • 心知为翘儿 - Reveals the old man's insight.
  • 不敢泄也 - Shows a sense of caution.
  • 归告督府曰 - Indicates a formal communication.
  • 贼未可图也 - Strategic assessment.
  • 第所爱幸玉夫人者 - Introduces a key figure.
  • 臣视之有外心 - Reveals a suspicion.
  • 当借以磔贼耳 - Suggests a plan.
  • 督府曰：“善。” - Shows approval.
  • 乃更遣罗中书诣海说降 - Indicates a new strategy.
  • 而益市金珠宝玉以阴贿翘儿 - Shows a covert operation.
  • 翘儿日夜在帐中 - Emphasizes his dedication.
  • 从容言 - Indicates a composed demeanor.
  • 大事必不成 - Prediction.
  • 不如降也 - Recommendation.
  • 江南苦兵久矣 - Provides context.
  • 降且得官，终身当共富贵 - Promise of future benefits.
  • 海遂许 - Concludes the negotiation.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 会督府 - The governor-general's office, a high-ranking administrative body.
  • 华老人 - An old man named Hua, likely a respected figure in the community.
  • 翘儿 - A character who plays a significant role in the narrative.
  • 玉夫人 - Lady Yu, a key figure with ulterior motives.
  • 江南 - The southern region, historically known for its cultural and economic significance.

Proper Nouns

  • 会督府 - The governor-general's office.
  • 华老人 - The old man Hua.
  • 翘儿 - Qiao'er.
  • 玉夫人 - Lady Yu.
  • 江南 - The southern region.

[1414]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 约降 - To agree to surrender.
  • 佯称逆降 - Claimed to surrender as a ruse.
  • 生致翘儿 - Captured Qiao'er alive.
  • 夜半投江死 - Drowned himself in the river at midnight.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 罗中书 - The secretary of the provincial government, likely a high-ranking official.
  • 督府 - The military governor, a high-ranking military official.
  • 海寨 - A sea fortress, likely a coastal military installation.
  • 翘儿 - A person, likely a military leader or advisor.
  • 倭人 - Refers to Japanese people, indicating a conflict with Japanese forces.
  • 永顺酋长 - The chief of Yongshun, likely a local tribal leader.
  • 钱塘 - Refers to Qiantang, a city in Zhejiang Province.
  • 明山 - Likely a historical figure, possibly a previous leader or benefactor of Qiao'er.

Proper Nouns

  • 罗中书 - The secretary of the provincial government.
  • 督府 - The military governor.
  • 海寨 - The sea fortress.
  • 翘儿 - A person, likely a military leader or advisor.
  • 倭人 - Japanese people.
  • 永顺酋长 - The chief of Yongshun.
  • 钱塘 - The city of Qiantang.
  • 明山 - A historical figure, possibly a previous leader or benefactor of Qiao'er.

[1415]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 外史氏曰 - Formal expression used by historians or chroniclers to introduce their commentary.
  • 海上之缗绅先生 - Refers to prominent scholars or officials living by the sea, with '缗绅' indicating their high social status.
  • 多能道翘儿死事 - Means 'many can recount the story of Qiao'er's death,' where '道' means 'to recount' and '死事' refers to 'dying in service.'
  • 得之华老人口云 - Means 'as told by an elderly man named Hua,' indicating the source of the information.
  • 李陵陷虏 - Refers to Li Ling's capture by the Xiongnu, a significant event in Chinese history.
  • 欲乘匈奴之间为汉内应 - Means 'to take advantage of the Xiongnu's internal divisions to serve as an insider for Han,' referring to Li Ling's attempt to act as a spy.
  • 迄无成立 - Means 'but ultimately failed to achieve anything,' indicating the failure of Li Ling's plan.
  • 溃其家声 - Means 'ruined his family's reputation,' highlighting the negative consequences of Li Ling's actions.
  • 翘儿以一贱娼 - Refers to Qiao'er, a lowly courtesan, emphasizing her humble background.
  • 能审于顺逆 - Means 'was able to discern right from wrong,' indicating her moral clarity.
  • 身陷不测 - Means 'found herself in a dangerous situation,' describing the perilous circumstances she faced.
  • 竟灭贼以报国 - Means 'eventually defeated the bandits to serve the country,' highlighting her heroic actions.
  • 诚伟烈矣 - Means 'truly a great and heroic deed,' expressing admiration for her actions.
  • 大史公曰 - Another formal expression used by historians or chroniclers to introduce their commentary.
  • 祸之生由爱姬殖 - Means 'the disaster originated from the love affair,' referring to the cause of the tragedy.
  • 海之谓也 - Means 'referring to the sea,' indicating the specific context of the disaster.
  • 翘之卒死以殉海 - Means 'Qiao'er ultimately died to sacrifice herself for the sea,' emphasizing her ultimate sacrifice.
  • 其或可附于堕楼之义也乎 - Means 'perhaps it can be compared to the virtue of jumping from a tower,' drawing a parallel to a similar act of self-sacrifice.
  • 王幼玉记 - Indicates the authorship of the text, identifying the narrator as Wang Youyu.
  • 王氏名真姬，字仙才，小字幼玉 - Provides the full name and aliases of the narrator, Wang Youyu.
  • 本京师人，随父流落于衡州 - Means 'originally from the capital, she followed her father to settle in Hengzhou,' providing her background.
  • 女弟女兄三人，皆为名娼 - Means 'her three sisters, both older and younger, were all famous courtesans,' describing her family.
  • 而其颜色歌舞，角于伦辈之上 - Means 'but her beauty and singing and dancing skills surpassed those of her peers,' highlighting her exceptional talents.
  • 群妓亦不敢与之争高下 - Means 'other courtesans were not brave enough to compete with her,' emphasizing her superiority.
  • 幼玉又出于弟兄之上 - Means 'Youyu was even more outstanding among her siblings,' further emphasizing her exceptional status.
  • 所与往还，皆衣冠士大夫 - Means 'the people she associated with were all scholars and officials,' indicating her high social standing.
  • 舍此虽巨商富贾，不能动其意 - Means 'even if there were wealthy merchants, they could not sway her,' highlighting her independence and integrity.
  • 夏公酉游衡阳，郡侯开宴召之 - Means 'Xia Gongyou traveled to Hengyang, and the local governor invited him to a banquet,' setting the scene.
  • 公酉曰 - Means 'Xia Gongyou said,' introducing his speech.
  • 闻衡阳有歌妓名王幼玉，妙歌舞，美颜色，孰是也 - Means 'I have heard that there is a courtesan named Wang Youyu in Hengyang, who is skilled in singing and dancing and beautiful in appearance, who is she,' expressing Xia Gongyou's curiosity.
  • 郡侯张郎中纪，乃命幼玉出拜 - Means 'The governor, Zhang Langzhong Ji, then ordered Youyu to come out and pay respects,' describing the interaction.
  • 公酉见之，嗟吁曰 - Means 'Xia Gongyou saw her and sighed, saying,' introducing his reaction.
  • 使汝居东西二京，未必在名妓之下 - Means 'If you were in the eastern or western capitals, you might not be inferior to the famous courtesans,' expressing admiration.
  • 反居于此，其名不得闻于天下 - Means 'Instead, you live here, and your name is not known throughout the world,' expressing regret.
  • 因命左右取笺为诗，赠幼玉曰 - Means 'He then ordered his attendants to fetch a writing pad and composed a poem to give to Youyu,' describing the gift.
  • 真宰无私心，万物逞殊形 - A line from the poem, meaning 'The true sovereign is impartial, and all things display their unique forms,' expressing a philosophical sentiment.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 外史氏 - Refers to a historian or chronicler, often used in classical Chinese texts to introduce commentary.
  • 李陵 - A famous Han general who was captured by the Xiongnu, a significant figure in Chinese history.
  • 匈奴 - The Xiongnu, a nomadic people who were often at odds with the Han dynasty.
  • 翘儿 - A fictional character, a lowly courtesan who becomes a hero.
  • 华老人 - An elderly man named Hua, who is the source of the story.
  • 王幼玉 - The narrator of the text, a courtesan with exceptional talents and a high social standing.
  • 衡州 - A city in present-day Hunan Province, China.
  • 夏公酉 - A scholar or official who travels to Hengzhou and meets Wang Youyu.
  • 郡侯 - A local governor or official.
  • 衣冠士大夫 - Refers to scholars and officials, indicating a high social status.
  • 巨商富贾 - Refers to wealthy merchants, indicating a different social class.
  • 东西二京 - Refers to the eastern and western capitals of the Han dynasty, which were Luoyang and Chang'an, respectively.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • This sentence introduces the historian's commentary and provides the source of the information.
  • This sentence describes Li Ling's capture and his failed attempt to serve as an insider for Han, leading to the ruin of his family's reputation.
  • This sentence praises Qiao'er for her moral clarity and heroic actions.
  • This sentence comments on the tragedy and draws a parallel to a similar act of self-sacrifice.
  • This phrase introduces the narrator, Wang Youyu.
  • This phrase provides the full name and aliases of the narrator.
  • This phrase describes the narrator's background.
  • This phrase describes the narrator's family.
  • This phrase highlights the narrator's exceptional talents.
  • This phrase emphasizes the narrator's superiority.
  • This phrase further emphasizes the narrator's exceptional status.
  • This phrase indicates the narrator's high social standing.
  • This phrase highlights the narrator's independence and integrity.
  • This phrase sets the scene for the interaction between Xia Gongyou and the local governor.
  • This phrase introduces Xia Gongyou's speech.
  • This phrase expresses Xia Gongyou's curiosity about Wang Youyu.
  • This phrase describes the interaction between the local governor and Wang Youyu.
  • This phrase introduces Xia Gongyou's reaction.
  • This phrase expresses admiration.
  • This phrase expresses regret.
  • This phrase describes the gift.

[1416]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 嗟尔 - Expression of sorrow or pity, often used to address someone or something.
  • 兰蕙质 - Refers to a person of noble character, comparing them to the fragrant orchid (*lan*) and lily (*hui*).
  • 幽谷青 - Describes a secluded, green valley, symbolizing a peaceful and untouched environment.
  • 清风暗助秀 - Means 'the gentle breeze silently aids in its beauty,' suggesting that external factors enhance the inherent qualities of the subject.
  • 雨露濡其泠 - Means 'the rain and dew moisten it, making it cool and fresh,' emphasizing the nourishment and freshness of the subject.
  • 一朝居上苑 - Means 'one day it resides in the imperial garden,' indicating a significant change in status or environment.
  • 桃李让芬馨 - Means 'the peach and plum trees yield their fragrance,' suggesting that the subject surpasses others in beauty or virtue.
  • 益有光 - Means 'it gains more brilliance,' indicating an increase in value or recognition.
  • 幼玉暇日 - Means 'the young jade during leisure time,' referring to a person of high moral character who has free time.
  • 常幽艳愁寂 - Means 'often lonely and melancholic,' describing a state of solitude and sadness.
  • 含芳未吐 - Means 'holding its fragrance but not yet blooming,' symbolizing potential that has not yet been realized.
  • 兹我夫也 - Means 'this is my husband,' expressing a strong personal connection.
  • 吾不舍子 - Means 'I will not let you go,' indicating a firm decision or commitment.
  • 讼子于官府 - Means 'I will sue you in court,' threatening legal action.
  • 异时幸有终身之约 - Means 'in the future, we will have a lifelong promise,' referring to a future commitment.
  • 吾之骨，异日当附子之先垄 - Means 'my bones will one day be buried in your ancestral grave,' expressing a deep desire to be with the person forever.
  • 我发委地，宝之若玉 - Means 'my hair hangs down to the ground, valued like jade,' emphasizing the beauty and value of the person.
  • 他人无敢窥觇 - Means 'no one dares to look at it,' indicating the person's uniqueness and the respect they command.
  • 于子无所惜 - Means 'I have nothing to hide from you,' expressing complete trust and openness.
  • 自解鬟剪一缕以遗富 - Means 'she cut a strand of her hair and gave it to Fu,' symbolizing a deep emotional bond.
  • 羁思不得会 - Means 'longing and inability to meet,' describing a state of longing and separation.
  • 忧枕 - Means 'worried and unable to sleep,' indicating deep concern or distress.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 兰蕙质 - A classical metaphor for noble character, often used in poetry and literature.
  • 幽谷青 - A common image in classical Chinese literature, symbolizing a peaceful and untouched environment.
  • 清风暗助秀 - A poetic description of natural beauty being enhanced by external factors.
  • 雨露濡其泠 - A poetic description of the nourishment and freshness provided by nature.
  • 一朝居上苑 - A reference to the imperial garden, symbolizing a significant change in status or environment.
  • 桃李让芬馨 - A metaphor for the subject surpassing others in beauty or virtue.
  • 益有光 - A metaphor for gaining more recognition or value.
  • 幼玉暇日 - A reference to a person of high moral character who has free time.
  • 常幽艳愁寂 - A description of a state of solitude and sadness, often used in classical Chinese poetry.
  • 含芳未吐 - A metaphor for potential that has not yet been realized.
  • 兹我夫也 - A strong personal connection, often used in classical Chinese literature.
  • 吾不舍子 - A firm decision or commitment, often used in classical Chinese literature.
  • 讼子于官府 - A threat of legal action, often used in classical Chinese literature.
  • 异时幸有终身之约 - A future commitment, often used in classical Chinese literature.
  • 吾之骨，异日当附子之先垄 - A deep desire to be with the person forever, often used in classical Chinese literature.
  • 我发委地，宝之若玉 - An emphasis on the beauty and value of the person, often used in classical Chinese literature.
  • 他人无敢窥觇 - A respect for the person's uniqueness, often used in classical Chinese literature.
  • 于子无所惜 - Complete trust and openness, often used in classical Chinese literature.
  • 自解鬟剪一缕以遗富 - A symbol of a deep emotional bond, often used in classical Chinese literature.
  • 羁思不得会 - A state of longing and separation, often used in classical Chinese literature.
  • 忧枕 - Deep concern or distress, often used in classical Chinese literature.

Proper Nouns

  • 幼玉 - The name of the subject.
  • 柳富 - The name of the character.
  • 润卿 - The courtesy name of the character.
  • 东都 - The eastern capital, likely referring to Luoyang.
  • 江上 - Refers to a location on the river.

[1417]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 紫府楼阁 - Refers to the Purple Palace, a mythical or celestial palace in Daoist and Buddhist traditions.
  • 金碧户牖 - Describes windows and doors adorned with gold and precious stones.
  • 红晖起 - Means 'the red light rises,' suggesting a glowing or radiant appearance.
  • 绝世妖姿 - Refers to an extraordinarily beautiful and alluring appearance.
  • 偶然思念起尘心 - Means 'occasionally thinking of earthly desires.'
  • 几年谪向衡阳市 - Means 'exiled to the city of Hengyang for several years.'
  • 娇烧飞下九天来 - A poetic expression meaning 'flying down from the nine heavens like a delicate flame.'
  • 长在娼家偶然耳 - Means 'it's just a matter of chance that she lives in a brothel.'
  • 天姿材色拟绝伦 - Means 'her natural beauty and talent are unparalleled.'
  • 压倒花衢众罗绮 - Means 'she surpasses all the beautiful women in the flower-filled streets.'
  • 绀发浓堆巫峡云 - Describes dark blue hair piled up like clouds over the Wu Gorge.
  • 翠眸横剪秋江水 - Describes eyes as green as the autumn river.
  • 素水纤长细细圆 - Describes hands as slender and delicate.
  • 春笋脱向青烟里 - A metaphor for hands emerging from the green mist.
  • 缓步莲花窄窄弓 - Describes walking with small, graceful steps.
  • 凤头翘起红裙底 - Describes the hem of the red skirt lifting slightly.
  • 桃花无颜乱红委 - Means 'peaches lose their color and fall in confusion.'
  • 王孙送目以劳魂 - Means 'princes send their eyes, wearying their souls.'
  • 东邻一见还羞死 - Means 'even a neighbor would be ashamed to see her.'
  • 自此城中豪富儿 - Means 'from this point on, wealthy and noble young men in the city.'
  • 呼童控马相追随 - Means 'calling for servants to control horses and follow her.'
  • 千金买得歌一曲 - Means 'buying a song for a thousand gold pieces.'
  • 暮雨朝云镇相续 - Means 'evening rain and morning clouds continue endlessly.'
  • 皇都年少是柳君 - Means 'the young man in the imperial capital is Liu Jun.'
  • 体段风流万事足 - Means 'his character and demeanor are graceful and complete.'
  • 幼玉一见苦留心 - Means 'You Yu, upon seeing her, was deeply moved.'
  • 殷勤厚遣行人嘱 - Means 'he sent someone to convey his earnest instructions.'
  • 青羽飞来洞户前 - Means 'green feathers flew to the entrance of the cave.'
  • 惟郎苦恨多拘束 - Means 'only the young man lamented the many constraints.'
  • 偷身不使父母知 - Means 'she secretly went out without her parents knowing.'
  • 江亭暗共才郎宿 - Means 'they secretly stayed together in a pavilion by the river.'
  • 犹恐恩情未甚坚 - Means 'still fearing that their love was not very strong.'
  • 解开鬟髻对郎前 - Means 'untying her hairpins in front of the young man.'
  • 一缕云随金剪断 - Means 'a strand of cloud-like hair was cut by golden scissors.'
  • 两心浓密更如绵 - Means 'their hearts were even more closely bound than silk.'
  • 自古美事多磨隔 - Means 'since ancient times, beautiful things are often tested by obstacles.'
  • 别时两意空悬悬 - Means 'when they parted, their feelings hung in the air.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 紫府楼阁 - Refers to the Purple Palace, a mythical or celestial palace in Daoist and Buddhist traditions.
  • 衡阳市 - Refers to the city of Hengyang, known for its beauty and culture.
  • 九天 - Refers to the nine heavens, a concept in Chinese mythology and Daoism.
  • 娼家 - Refers to a brothel or a place where courtesans live.
  • 花衢 - Refers to a street filled with flowers, often used to describe a beautiful and prosperous area.
  • 巫峡 - Refers to the Wu Gorge, a famous scenic spot along the Yangtze River.
  • 皇都 - Refers to the imperial capital, often used to describe the center of power and culture.
  • 柳君 - Refers to Liu Jun, a young man in the imperial capital.
  • 幼玉 - Refers to You Yu, a character in the story.
  • 江亭 - Refers to a pavilion by the river, often used as a place for romantic encounters.
  • 鬟髻 - Refers to traditional hairstyles for women, often adorned with hairpins.
  • 青羽 - Refers to green feathers, often associated with birds or mythical creatures.
  • 洞户 - Refers to the entrance of a cave, often used in mythological contexts.
  • 暮雨朝云 - Refers to the natural phenomena of evening rain and morning clouds, often used metaphorically to describe fleeting or transient things.

Proper Nouns

  • 紫府 - The Purple Palace.
  • 衡阳市 - The city of Hengyang.
  • 九天 - The nine heavens.
  • 娼家 - A brothel or a place where courtesans live.
  • 花衢 - A street filled with flowers.
  • 巫峡 - The Wu Gorge.
  • 皇都 - The imperial capital.
  • 柳君 - Liu Jun, a young man in the imperial capital.
  • 幼玉 - You Yu, a character in the story.
  • 江亭 - A pavilion by the river.
  • 鬟髻 - Traditional hairstyles for women.
  • 青羽 - Green feathers.
  • 洞户 - The entrance of a cave.

[1418]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 清宵長歎明月下 - Poetic expression meaning 'in the clear night, sighing under the moon.' Conveys a sense of melancholy and introspection.
  • 花時洒泪东风前 - Metaphorical phrase meaning 'shedding tears in the east wind during the flowering season.' Suggests a poignant moment of sadness amidst beauty.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 誰怜潘果空投，贾香难与，愁肠安托 - Complex phrase expressing sorrow and longing. '谁怜' (shéi lián) means 'who pities,' '潘果空投' (pān guǒ kōng tóu) is unclear but likely refers to a metaphorical or symbolic gesture, '贾香难与' (jiǎ xiāng nán yǔ) suggests difficulty in sharing or communicating, and '愁肠安托' (chóu cháng ān tuō) means 'where to entrust one's sorrowful heart.'
  • 带眼轻拴，须看取杨柳腰肢如削 - '带眼轻拴' (dài yǎn qīng shuān) means 'lightly fastened with a belt,' '须看取' (xū kàn qǔ) means 'must see,' '杨柳腰肢如削' (yáng liǔ yāo zhī rú xuē) is a poetic description of a slender waist like that of willow.
  • 珠履玲珑，罗衫雅淡，件件无心着 - '珠履玲珑' (zhū lǚ líng lóng) means 'jeweled shoes that are delicate,' '罗衫雅淡' (luó shān yǎ dàn) means 'silk robes that are elegant and subdued,' '件件无心着' (jiàn jiàn wú xīn zhe) means 'each piece is worn without thought.'
  • 何时厮见，得偿今日萧索 - '何时厮见' (hé shí sī jiàn) means 'when will we meet again,' '得偿今日萧索' (dé cháng jīn rì xiāo suǒ) means 'to make up for today's loneliness.'
  • 花径争穿，珠帘屡认，正逢梅雨芹泥润 - '花径争穿' (huā jìng zhēng chuān) means 'competing to walk through the flower path,' '珠帘屡认' (zhū lián lǚ rèn) means 'often recognizing the pearl curtains,' '正逢梅雨芹泥润' (zhèng féng méi yǔ qín ní rùn) means 'just encountering the rainy season when the soil is moist.'
  • 画梁无处可安巢，玉纤为把花枝衬 - '画梁无处可安巢' (huà liáng wú chù kě ān cháo) means 'there is no place to build a nest on the painted beam,' '玉纤为把花枝衬' (yù xiān wèi bǎ huā zhī chèn) means 'the delicate hands hold up the flower branches.'
  • 社日才来，端阳已近，寻巢为甚偏迟钝 - '社日才来' (shè rì cái lái) means 'the *Shè Rì* festival has just arrived,' '端阳已近' (duān yáng yǐ jìn) means 'the *Duān Yáng* festival is approaching,' '寻巢为甚偏迟钝' (xún cháo wéi shén piān chí dùn) means 'why is finding a nest so slow?'
  • 算来一似凤鸾期，跎蛇渐觉无真信 - '算来一似凤鸾期' (suàn lái yī sì fèng luán qī) means 'it seems like the time of the phoenix and crane,' '跎蛇渐觉无真信' (tuó shé jiàn jué wú zhēn xìn) means 'the snake gradually feels no true sign.'
  • 昨夜惊眠梅雨大，枕前窗上频敲，天明翻觉梦魂遥 - '昨夜惊眠梅雨大' (zuó yè jīng mián méi yǔ dà) means 'last night the heavy rain woke me from sleep,' '枕前窗上频敲' (zhěn qián chuāng shàng pín qiāo) means 'the raindrops frequently tap on the pillow and window,' '天明翻觉梦魂遥' (tiān míng fān jué mèng hún yáo) means 'at dawn, I realize my dream was far away.'
  • 起来看女伴，薰袖已香消 - '起来看女伴' (qǐ lái kàn nǚ bàn) means 'get up to see my female companions,' '薰袖已香消' (xūn xiù yǐ xiāng xiāo) means 'the fragrant sleeves have already faded.'
  • 云锁房栊烟锁竹，卷帘水湿鲛绡 - '云锁房栊烟锁竹' (yún suǒ fáng lóng yān suǒ zhú) means 'clouds lock the windows, smoke locks the bamboo,' '卷帘水湿鲛绡' (juǎn lián shuǐ shī jiāo xiāo) means 'the water wets the curtains.'
  • 菱花低照拂眉梢，玉梳云发润，不喜上兰膏 - '菱花低照拂眉梢' (líng huā dī zhào fú méi shāo) means 'the dim light of the mirror reflects on the eyebrows,' '玉梳云发润' (yù shū yún fā rùn) means 'the jade comb smooths the cloud-like hair,' '不喜上兰膏' (bù xǐ shàng lán gāo) means 'I don't like applying the fragrant oil.'

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sequence '闻已，归告奴仆，乘马便走' must be read as a single, continuous narrative.
  • The phrase '衣服悉弃于厅中' is elliptical but clear in meaning.
  • The long sequence '至夜，已行五六十里，出县界，止宿村店' must be read as a single, continuous narrative.
  • The clause '仆从但怪奔走，不知何故' can be read as two linked ideas: the servants were surprised by the guest's actions and did not know the reason.
  • The sequence '此人歇定，乃言此贼负心之状，言讫吁嗟，奴仆悉涕泣之次' must be read as a single, continuous narrative.
  • The sudden appearance '忽床下一人，持匕首出立' must be read as a dramatic moment.
  • The reaction '此客大惧' is a direct response to the previous event.
  • The dialogue '我，义士也。宰使我来取君头' must be read as a single, continuous narrative.
  • The realization '适闻说，方知此宰负心，不然枉杀贤士' must be read as a single, continuous narrative.
  • The promise '吾义不舍此人也。公且勿睡，少顷与君取此宰头，以雪公冤' must be read as a single, continuous narrative.
  • The reaction '此人怕惧愧谢' is a direct response to the previous event.
  • The action '剑客持剑，出门如飞' must be read as a single, continuous narrative.
  • The sequence '二更已至，呼曰：“贼首至”' must be read as a single, continuous narrative.
  • The sequence '命火观之，乃令头也' must be read as a single, continuous narrative.
  • The sequence '剑客辞诀，不知所之' must be read as a single, continuous narrative.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 贾随诘问 - 'Jia followed up with questioning.' '诘问' means to question or interrogate.
  • 不能隐，具有始未告之 - 'Could not hide, and thus told the full beginning and end.' '始未' refers to the beginning and the end, or the full story.
  • 果尔？吾岂不可往耶！ - 'If that is true, why can’t I go too?' A rhetorical question expressing disbelief and a challenge.
  • 如不吾同，当发其妖幻，首于官，且白某太守 - 'If you do not agree with me, I will report his sorcery to the authorities and also inform Lord So-and-so.' '首于官' means to report to the authorities; '白' means to inform or tell.
  • 甚惧，曰：“今日已暮矣。俟明日，同诣道人谋之。” - 'He was very afraid and said, 'Today is already late. Let us wait until tomorrow and go together to consult the Daoist.'
  • 拂旦往 - 'They went at dawn.' '拂旦' refers to the very early morning, just before sunrise.
  • 道人不悦，曰：“机已泄，恐不能神，当作别计。” - 'The Daoist was not pleased, saying, 'The secret has been revealed, so I fear it cannot remain magical anymore. I will have to make other plans.' '机' here refers to the secret or the magical trick.
  • 城西某家有园池之胜 - 'There is a family in the western part of the city with a beautiful garden and pond.' '园池之胜' means the beauty or excellence of the garden and pond.
  • 能从吾饮乎？ - 'Will you join me in drinking?' A polite invitation.
  • 一亭前有大假山 - 'Before a pavilion there was a large artificial hill.' '假山' is a garden feature, an artificial mountain.
  • 振衣起，举手指划山石，一峰中分 - 'He shook off his robe, rose up, and with a finger stroke, split the mountain in two.' '振衣' is a poetic way of saying to shake off one’s robe, often used to depict a sudden or dramatic action.
  • 两人就视，见楼台山水，花木靓丽，渔舟从溪上来，碧桃红杏缤纷 - 'The two looked and saw pavilions, mountains, rivers, lush flowers and trees, and fishing boats coming up from the stream, with peach and apricot blossoms in full bloom.' This is a vivid description of a magical scene.
  • 方注目间，道人登舟，其去如飞 - 'As they were still gazing, the Daoist boarded the boat, and departed like a bird.' '方注目间' means 'just as they were looking closely.'
  • 贾引袖力挽，石缝遽合，伤其指 - 'Jia pulled with his sleeve, trying to stop him, but the stone split closed suddenly, injuring his finger.' '遽合' is sudden closing or coming together.
  • 道人杳无踪矣 - 'The Daoist vanished without a trace.' '杳无踪' means completely disappeared or gone without a trace.
  • 他日，两人复至社坛，用前法施之，已无所效，惘然怨侮而归 - 'On another day, the two returned to the ancestral altar and tried the same method again, but it had no effect. They returned in confusion and resentment.' '惘然' means confused or lost; '怨侮' means to resent or blame.
  • 后访乳医尝出入大守家者，使密叩，姬云：“梦中恍惚与一男子宴私。今久不复然矣。” - 'Later, they consulted a midwife who had visited the governor’s household, and asked her in secret. The woman said, 'In a dream I was vaguely with a man in a private feast. Now I have not had such dreams for a long time.' '乳医' refers to a midwife or a woman skilled in childbirth; '宴私' refers to a private banquet or intimate gathering.'

  • 梦中恍惚与一男子宴私 - A reference to a dream involving a private or intimate encounter, which may reflect classical Chinese themes of dreams as omens or supernatural experiences.

  • 起视之，则风雨晦冥 - Means 'he got up to look, and there was wind and rain, with darkness.' The term '晦冥' describes a state of darkness or obscurity.
  • 缠绕此树，电光晃耀，忽上忽下 - Means 'it was wrapped around the tree, with lightning flashing up and down.' The term '晃耀' describes the flickering or flashing of light.
  • 至明，有一大蛇，紫鳞赤尾，绿头白角，额上有王字，身被数创，死于树 - Means 'by morning, there was a large snake, with purple scales and a red tail, green head and white horns, a 'Wang' character on its forehead, and several wounds on its body, dead on the tree.' The description is highly detailed and symbolic.

  • 妖怪遂绝 - Means 'the ghosts and demons were thus eliminated.' The term '遂绝' indicates the final result.

  • 县门 - Refers to the gate of the county, a significant location in ancient Chinese administrative structures.

[1511]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 望之，不暇他视 - Vivid expression meaning 'to look at it, one has no time to look elsewhere,' emphasizing the captivating nature of the scene.
  • 尘外境也 - Literally 'a realm beyond the dust,' a metaphor for a transcendent or otherworldly place.
  • 真所谓 - Means 'truly what is called,' introducing a definitive statement.
  • 使人揖入 - Means 'having someone bow and lead in,' indicating formal hospitality.
  • 既入，见亭上有神仙十数 - Means 'once inside, one sees several dozen immortals on the pavilion,' describing a fantastical scene.
  • 皆极色也 - Means 'all of them are of the utmost beauty,' emphasizing the immortals' attractiveness.
  • 凝立若仁 - Means 'standing motionless as if benevolent,' describing the immortals' serene demeanor.
  • 半掉云袂 - Means 'half-draped in cloud sleeves,' evoking a mystical and ethereal appearance.
  • 飘飘然 - Means 'floating lightly,' suggesting an otherworldly quality.
  • 纹绣杳渺 - Means 'patterns and embroidery are faint and distant,' describing the immortals' attire as indistinct and mysterious.
  • 殆不可识 - Means 'almost impossible to recognize,' emphasizing the immortals' otherness.
  • 谢曰 - Means 'he replied,' introducing the protagonist's response.
  • 不意今日得为刘阮 - Means 'I did not expect to become Liu and Ruan today,' expressing surprise and joy.
  • 幸何甚哉 - Means 'what great fortune it is!' emphasizing the protagonist's happiness.
  • 然则次为何所 - Means 'but what is this place?' seeking clarification.
  • 女郎又何为者 - Means 'and what are these ladies?' seeking further explanation.
  • 愿一闻知 - Means 'I wish to hear and know,' expressing a desire for information.
  • 我玉清之女也 - Means 'we are the daughters of the Jade Clear Palace,' identifying their origin.
  • 居于此久矣 - Means 'we have dwelt here for a long time,' emphasizing their longevity.
  • 此乃玉清宫也 - Means 'this is the Jade Clear Palace,' identifying the location.
  • 向闻君为下第进士 - Means 'earlier we heard that you were a scholar-official who failed the exams,' providing background.
  • 寓游至此 - Means 'who came here to wander,' describing the protagonist's presence.
  • 将以一言奉请 - Means 'we intended to invite you with a single word,' explaining their initial hesitation.
  • 又惧君子不顾 - Means 'but feared that you might not care,' expressing their concern.
  • 且贻其辱 - Means 'and might bring shame upon us,' emphasizing the potential consequence.

  • 果副吾志 - Means 'which indeed fulfilled our wish,' indicating satisfaction.
  • 虽然 - Means 'though,' introducing a contrast.
  • 此仙府也 - Means 'this is a celestial abode,' identifying the place.
  • 虽云不可滞 - Means 'though it is said one cannot linger here,' emphasizing the transient nature.
  • 世间人君居之 - Means 'even if an earthly ruler were to reside here,' introducing a hypothetical.
  • 固无损耳 - Means 'it would certainly cause no harm,' concluding the thought.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 刘阮事 - Refers to the famous legend of Liu and Ruan, who encountered immortals in a cave and were offered an immortal drink, causing them to age rapidly upon returning to the mortal world.
  • 玉清 - Refers to the Jade Clear Palace, a celestial abode in Taoist cosmology.
  • 下第进士 - Refers to a scholar-official who failed the imperial examinations, indicating a lower social status.
  • 郑氏之亭 - Refers to a pavilion belonging to the Vang family, likely a place of significance in the narrative.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The opening sequence '望之，不暇他视，真所谓尘外境也' must be read as a single descriptive statement, emphasizing the scene's captivating and otherworldly nature.
  • The long descriptive clause '既入，见亭上有神仙十数，皆极色也，凝立若仁，半掉云袂，飘飘然' must be read as a single vision of the immortals, emphasizing their beauty and ethereal appearance.
  • The clause '纹绣杳渺，殆不可识' must be read as a single description of the immortals' attire, emphasizing its indistinct and mysterious nature.
  • The clause '群仙喜曰：“君不闻刘阮事乎？今日亦如是。愿奉一醉，将尽春色。君以为何如？”' must be read as a single speech from the immortals, introducing their welcoming toast.
  • The clause '谢曰：“不意今日得为刘阮，幸何甚哉。然则次为何所，女郎又何为者，愿一闻知。”' must be read as a single response from the protagonist, expressing his surprise and seeking information.
  • The long clause '我玉清之女也，居于此久矣。此乃玉清宫也。向闻君为下第进士，寓游至此，将以一言奉请，又惧君子不顾，且贻其辱，是人假郑氏之亭以命君，果副吾志。虽然，此仙府也。虽云不可滞，世间人君居之，固无损耳。' must be read as a single explanation from the immortals, providing background and context.

Proper Nouns

  • 刘阮 - The legendary figures Liu and Ruan.
  • 玉清 - The Jade Clear Palace, a celestial abode.
  • 郑氏之亭 - The pavilion belonging to the Vang family.

  • 后知诚意，大惊 - Means 'later, knowing his sincerity, they were greatly surprised.' '后知' means 'later, knowing,' '诚意' means 'sincerity,' and '大惊' means 'greatly surprised.'
  • 其老胡亦有泣者 - Means 'some of the older Huns even cried.' '老胡' means 'older Huns,' and '泣者' means 'those who cried.'
  • 众遂请市此宝 - Means 'the crowd then asked to buy this treasure.' '遂' means 'then,' '请' means 'asked,' and '市此宝' means 'to buy this treasure.'
  • 生大言索百万 - Means 'the student boldly demanded a million.' '大言' means 'boldly demanded,' and '索百万' means 'demanded a million.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 胡客 - Refers to a truce or treaty with the Huns, a nomadic group that frequently interacted with Chinese dynasties.
  • 宝会 - A formal event where treasures are displayed and valued, similar to a modern-day exhibition or auction.
  • 明珠 - Large, valuable pearls, often associated with wealth and status.

  • 太学中诸生闻之 - Means 'the students at the Imperial Academy heard about it,' referring to the students studying at the national university.
  • 彼胡率爱利，不顾其身 - Means 'those barbarians are usually greedy and don't care about themselves,' criticizing the behavior of the barbarians.
  • 争盐菜之微，尚致相贼杀者 - Means 'they even fight over small things like salt and vegetables, leading to mutual slaughter,' highlighting the barbarians' savagery.
  • 宁肯弃金缯为子寿乎 - Means 'would they really give up gold and silk as gifts for your longevity?' questioning the sincerity of their offer.
  • 何为独厚君耶 - Means 'why are they treating you so specially?' expressing surprise at the special treatment.
  • 是必有故 - Means 'there must be a reason,' suggesting that there is an underlying motive.
  • 君宜匿身郊野间，以避之也 - Means 'you should hide yourself in the suburbs to avoid them,' advising caution.
  • 侨居渭上 - Means 'live in exile on the banks of the Wei River,' indicating a change in residence.
  • 杜门不出 - Means 'shut the door and do not go out,' describing a reclusive lifestyle.
  • 群胡又诣其门 - Means 'the group of barbarians came to his door again,' indicating a return visit.
  • 我未得尽言 - Means 'I couldn't fully explain myself,' suggesting that there was something left unsaid.
  • 今君退居郊野，果吾心也 - Means 'now that you have retreated to the suburbs, it is exactly what I wanted,' expressing satisfaction.
  • 我之来，非偶然也，盖有求君耳 - Means 'my coming here is not accidental; I have something to ask of you,' indicating a specific purpose.
  • 幸望诺之 - Means 'I hope you will agree,' making a request.
  • 且我所祈，于君，固无害；于我，则大惠也 - Means 'what I ask of you is harmless to you but very beneficial to me,' explaining the mutual benefit.
  • 谨受教 - Means 'I will follow your advice,' showing willingness to comply.
  • 吾子好食面乎 - Means 'do you like eating noodles?' asking about a personal preference.
  • 食面者非君也，乃君腹中一虫耳 - Means 'the one who eats noodles is not you, but a worm in your stomach,' a metaphorical statement.
  • 今我欲以一粒药进君，君饵之，当吐出虫，则我以厚价从君易之，其可乎 - Means 'now I want to give you a pill. If you take it, you will vomit out the worm, and I will pay a high price to buy it from you,' proposing a deal.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 长安 - Refers to Chang'an, the capital city of the Han Dynasty, known for its grandeur and importance.
  • 太学 - Refers to the Imperial Academy, the highest institution of learning in ancient China.
  • 金缯 - Refers to gold and silk, valuable gifts often given in ancient China.
  • 胡人 - Refers to people from the northern and western regions, often associated with nomadic tribes.
  • 渭上 - Refers to the banks of the Wei River, a significant location in Chinese history.

Proper Nouns

  • 执其本而取其未，其远乎哉 - Means 'To grasp its essence and extract its potential, how far is that?'
  • 以筒盛虫，又以金函扃之 - Means 'To place the worm in a tube and then seal it in a golden box.'
  • 明日当即来 - Means 'I will come tomorrow.'
  • 以十两重辇金玉绢帛，约数万 - Means 'with a cartload of gold, jade, silk, and cloth, totaling several ten thousand taels.'
  • 共持金函而去 - Means 'they left together with the golden box.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 吾子能与我偕游海中乎？我欲探海中之奇宝，以耀天下，而吾子岂非好奇之士耶？ - Can you accompany us on a journey to the sea? We wish to explore the strange treasures of the sea to dazzle the world, and aren't you a man of curiosity?
  • 胡人结宇而居，于是置油膏于银鼎中，构火其下，投虫于鼎中，炼之七日不绝燎 - The Hu people built a dwelling and placed oil and fat in a silver cauldron, setting fire beneath it, and threw insects into the cauldron, refining them for seven days without ceasing the flame.
  • 忽有一童，分发，衣青襦，自海水中出，捧白盘。盘中有径寸珠甚多，来献胡人 - Suddenly, a boy appeared, with parted hair, wearing a green robe, emerging from the sea water, carrying a white tray. The tray contained many pearls about an inch in diameter, which he presented to the Hu people.
  • 胡人大声叱之，其童色惧，捧盘而去 - The Hu people shouted fiercely at him, and the boy, frightened, carried the tray away.
  • 仅食顷，又有一玉女，貌极冶，衣露绡之衣，佩玉珠，翩翩自海中而出，捧紫玉盘，中有大珠数十，来献胡人 - After only a meal's time, another jade maiden appeared, extremely beautiful, wearing a translucent silk robe, adorned with jade and pearls, gracefully emerging from the sea, carrying a purple jade tray containing dozens of large pearls, which she presented to the Hu people.
  • 胡人骂之，玉女捧盘而去 - The Hu people cursed her, and the jade maiden carried the tray away.
  • 俄有一仙人，戴碧瑶冠，衣紫霞衣，捧绛帕籍盘，中有一珠，径二寸许，奇光泛彩，照数十步，仙人以献胡人，胡人笑而受之，喜谓曰：“至宝来矣。” - Soon, a fairy appeared, wearing a green jade crown and a purple mist robe, carrying a red silk tray containing a pearl about two inches in diameter, radiating a wondrous light that illuminated dozens of steps. The fairy presented it to the Hu people, who laughed and accepted it, happily saying, 'The ultimate treasure has arrived.'
  • 即命绝燎，自鼎中收虫，置金函中。其虫虽炼之且久，而跳跃如初 - They immediately ordered the fire to be extinguished, retrieved the insects from the cauldron, and placed them in a golden box. Despite being refined for so long, the insects still jumped as if they were new.
  • 胡人吞其珠，谓曰：“子随我入海中，慎无惧。” - The Hu people swallowed the pearl and said to him, 'Follow me into the sea, and do not be afraid.'

[1525]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 养就丹砂寿算绵 - Cultivated elixir of immortality ensures longevity; '养就' implies cultivation, '丹砂' refers to cinnabar, and '寿算绵' means longevity is assured.
  • 鸡群独出势昂然 - Standing out proudly among the chickens; '鸡群' refers to a group of chickens, and '独出势昂然' describes a bird standing out with a proud posture.
  • 数声唳月归三岛 - A few cries under the moon return to the three islands; '唳' refers to the cry of a bird, '月' is the moon, and '三岛' refers to the mythical islands of the immortals.
  • 几度乘风上九天 - Several times riding the wind to the ninth heaven; '乘风' means 'riding the wind,' and '九天' refers to the highest heavens in Chinese cosmology.
  • 长夜听琴来蕙帐 - Listening to the lute in the long night in the fragrant tent; '蕙帐' refers to a tent fragrant with orchids, often associated with immortals.
  • 清晨觅食在芝田 - Seeking food in the morning in the orchid fields; '芝田' refers to fields of orchids, symbolizing a paradise or the realm of immortals.
  • 自从华表归来后 - Since returning from the totem pole; '华表' is a totem pole or monument, often associated with immortals or long journeys.
  • 沧海桑田几变迁 - How many changes have occurred in the sea and fields; '沧海桑田' is a metaphor for great changes over time.
  • 读未毕，叟笑曰 - Having not finished reading, the old man laughed and said; '叟' refers to an old man.
  • 此老夫之自赞也 - This is my self-praise; '自赞' means 'self-praise' or 'self-description.'
  • 止宿其屋 - Stayed overnight in his house; '止宿' means 'to stay overnight.'
  • 翌日辞归 - The next day, he bid farewell and returned; '翌日' means 'the next day,' and '辞归' means 'to bid farewell and return.'
  • 携手送至旧路而别 - Held hands to send him to the old road and then parted; '携手' means 'to hold hands,' and '旧路' means 'the old road.'
  • 君行宜珍重，勿轻回顾 - You should be careful on your journey, do not lightly look back; '珍重' means 'to be careful,' and '勿轻回顾' means 'do not lightly look back.'
  • 始忆诗赞为鹤焉 - Only then did he remember that the poem praised a crane; '始忆' means 'only then did he remember,' and '鹤' means 'crane.'

  • 九天 - The highest heavens in Chinese cosmology, representing the pinnacle of existence.
  • 蕙帐 - A tent fragrant with orchids, often associated with immortals or a paradisiacal setting.
  • 芝田 - Fields of orchids, symbolizing a paradise or the realm of immortals.
  • 华表 - A totem pole or monument, often associated with immortals or long journeys.

[1526]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 采采珍禽世罕俦 - Onomatopoeic expression meaning 'to gather' or 'to collect,' describing the rarity and preciousness of the birds.

  • 丹心不改常同旧，翠羽相辉每共游 - Phrase emphasizing the birds' loyalty and frequent companionship: 'loyal heart unchanged always the same as before,' 'green feathers shine together often travel together.'
  • 齐瓦对眠金殿晚，点沙双蹲玉田秋 - Phrase describing the birds' serene and harmonious coexistence in different settings: 'together on the tiles sleep side by side golden palace evening,' 'dotting the sand sit side by side jade field autumn.'
  • 此生莫遣轻离别，交颈成双到白头 - Phrase expressing a wish for the birds to remain inseparable throughout their lives: 'in this life do not let easily separate,' 'neck-to-neck stay together until old age.'
  • 同盟三五共优游，镇日清闲得自由 - Phrase describing the companionship and freedom of the individuals: 'allies a few enjoy leisure together,' 'all day leisurely gain freedom.'
  • 片雪晴飞红蓼晚，玉衣寒映碧波秋 - Phrase painting a vivid picture of a serene autumn scene: 'a piece of snow flying in the clear sky red reed evening,' 'jade robe cold reflection green waves in autumn.'
  • 相亲相近来还去，无束无拘没又浮 - Phrase describing the close and unrestricted relationship between the individuals: 'close and affectionate coming and going,' 'unrestricted and free submerged and floating.'
  • 岁暮江湖谁是侣，忘机长伴钓鱼舟 - Phrase expressing a desire for a companion in the natural world: 'end of the year rivers and lakes who is a companion,' 'forgetting worldly concerns long to be a companion of the fishing boat.'
  • 予舟中无人，肯过访否 - Phrase asking if the individuals would visit the boat: 'I no one in the boat will visit whether.'
  • 极其戏滤 - Phrase describing the playful and flirtatious interaction: 'to the fullest extent playful and flirtatious.'
  • 两情甚浓 - Phrase describing the deep mutual affection: 'two hearts very deep.'
  • 倚翠偎红情最奇，巫山黯黯雨云迷 - Phrase describing the extraordinary and passionate love: 'leaning on green and hugging red most extraordinary feelings,' 'the dark clouds and rain of Mount Wu are bewildering.'
  • 风流好似偷香蝶，才过东来又向西 - Phrase comparing the individuals' charm and grace to butterflies that flit from one place to another: 'charming and graceful like stealing fragrant butterflies,' 'just coming from the east and then going west.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 采采珍禽世罕俦 - Poetic way of describing rare and precious birds, symbolizing beauty and rarity in classical Chinese literature.

  • 齐瓦对眠金殿晚，点沙双蹲玉田秋 - Setting of 'golden palace' and 'jade field' evoking a luxurious and serene environment, often used to describe the idealized world of the elite.
  • 此生莫遣轻离别，交颈成双到白头 - Phrase 'neck-to-neck stay together until old age' symbolizing a lifelong commitment and deep affection.
  • 同盟三五共优游，镇日清闲得自由 - Idea of 'a few allies' and 'enjoying leisure together' reflecting the value of friendship and leisure in classical Chinese culture.
  • 片雪晴飞红蓼晚，玉衣寒映碧波秋 - Imagery of 'a piece of snow' and 'jade robe' evoking a sense of elegance and serenity in classical Chinese poetry.
  • 相亲相近来还去，无束无拘没又浮 - Phrase 'unrestricted and free' reflecting the value of freedom and spontaneity in classical Chinese culture.
  • 岁暮江湖谁是侣，忘机长伴钓鱼舟 - Idea of 'forgetting worldly concerns' and 'long to be a companion of the fishing boat' reflecting the ideal of a simple and peaceful life in nature.

  • 极其戏滤 - Phrase 'playful and flirtatious' describing a lighthearted and romantic interaction.
  • 两情甚浓 - Phrase 'very deep mutual feelings' emphasizing the intensity of the emotional connection.
  • 倚翠偎红情最奇，巫山黯黯雨云迷 - Reference to 'Mount Wu' a well-known allusion in Chinese literature, often associated with the myth of the goddesses of Mount Wu and the story of the scholar and the goddess.
  • 风流好似偷香蝶，才过东来又向西 - Comparison to 'stealing fragrant butterflies' a poetic way of describing the charm and grace of the individuals.

  • 而搜古墓，复得数犬，毛状皆异 - 而 means 'and,' 搜 means 'searched,' 古墓 means 'ancient tomb,' 复得 means 'found again,' 数犬 means 'several dogs,' and 毛状皆异 means 'all had different fur patterns.' This phrase indicates that Master Han searched the ancient tomb and found several dogs, all with different fur patterns.

Proper Nouns

  • 韩生 - Refers to Master Han, the owner of the stable and the person who ultimately kills and eats the dog.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '祝归以白母' could be interpreted as 'Zhu Gui informed his mother' or 'Zhu Gui reported to his mother.'
  • The phrase '或曰' introduces a speculative statement, which could be rendered as 'Some said' or 'It was said that.'
  • The sentence '吾闻物久则妖' is a general statement about objects becoming spirits over time, which could be rendered as 'I have heard that objects become spirits after a long time.'
  • The sentence '君畜牝猪已过十年' describes the length of time the female pig has been kept, which could be rendered as 'Your female pig has been kept for over ten years.'
  • The sentence '其豚现在八九' describes the number of piglets, which could be rendered as 'Its piglets are now eight or nine.'
  • The sentence '皂其本色也' describes the pig's natural color, which could be rendered as 'It is in its natural color.'
  • The sentence '母然之' describes the mother's reaction, which could be rendered as 'The mother agreed.'
  • The sentence '议鬻诸屠肆' describes the discussion about selling the pig, which could be rendered as 'They discussed selling it to a butcher's shop.'
  • The sentence '真人与子诀曰' introduces the farewell speech, which could be rendered as 'The true person bid farewell to his son, saying...'
  • The question '相从有几' asks about the length of their relationship, which could be rendered as 'How long have we been together?'
  • The statement '冥缘遂绝' describes the end of their fateful connection, which could be rendered as 'Our fateful connection has ended.'
  • The statement '劝子自爱' advises the son to take care of himself, which could be rendered as 'I advise you to take care of yourself.'
  • The statement '无以我思' means 'Do not think of me.'
  • The sentence '言之涕泣而去' describes the speaker speaking with tears and then leaving, which could be rendered as 'He spoke with tears and then left.'

  • 一技独茂逞冰肌 - 'One branch alone flourishes, displaying its icy skin,' describing a single flower or branch that stands out in its purity and whiteness.
  • 万朵争妍含醉脸 - 'Ten thousand blossoms vie in beauty, each with a drunken face,' a metaphor for the beauty and abundance of flowers, as if they are inebriated with their own beauty.
  • 雪飘飘 - A repeated phrase describing falling snow, often used to evoke a cold, serene, or melancholic atmosphere.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The long sequence '已而夜静酒阑，彼此忘怀，笑语欢谑，挨肩携手，淫情各炽，遂入室解衣就寝，云情雨意，两相欢合，口送丁香，极尽绸缪' describes a series of actions and emotions, leading to a sexual encounter. The syntax is fluid and continuous, capturing the progression of events and feelings.
  • The placement of '一律' (a single poem) at the end of the sentence in '美人就枕上吟诗一律' is somewhat ambiguous but suggests that the woman recites a single poem.
  • The metaphorical expression '尽付湘江水上沤' in '追思向日繁华地，尽付湘江水上沤' is rich in imagery but requires careful translation to convey the intended meaning of the transient nature of life.
  • The use of '祸起萧墙' in '本欲与君共期偕老，不料上天降罚，祸起萧墙' adds a layer of complexity to the statement, indicating that the tragedy is caused by internal conflict.
  • The contrast between the present and the future in '今夕尽此一欢，明朝永别' is clear but requires a translation that captures the emotional weight of the statement.
  • The conditional structure in '君宜速避，不然祸且及君' is straightforward but the urgency and gravity of the warning must be conveyed effectively.

  • 今日岂得容易呈身哉 - Means 'Today, how could it be easy for her to appear?' expressing skepticism about the ease of the situation.
  • 老大蛾眉，琵琶哀怨 - Refers to an older woman with beautiful eyebrows playing the pipa with a sad and mournful tune, evoking a sense of nostalgia and sorrow.
  • 纵使尊前轻拢慢捻 - Means 'Even if she plays gently and slowly,' suggesting a delicate and refined performance.
  • 适足以增天涯沦落之感 - Means 'It would only add to the feeling of being lost in the world,' highlighting the emotional impact of the performance.
  • 岂能醉而成欢耶 - Means 'How could it lead to joy and drunkenness?' expressing doubt about the positive outcome.
  • 且静听之 - Means 'Let's listen quietly,' indicating a moment of patience and attentiveness.
  • 良久而寂 - Means 'After a long time, it became silent,' suggesting the end of the mysterious singing.
  • 酒罢回船 - Means 'After the wine was finished, they returned to their boat,' indicating the conclusion of the gathering.
  • 竟莫知其何故 - Means 'They still did not know why,' leaving the mystery unresolved.
  • 独韶迭宕 - Means 'Only Shao was versatile and passionate,' describing his character.
  • 好事多情 - Means 'He was fond of good things and had many emotions,' indicating his interest in experiences and feelings.
  • 翌日往究其实 - Means 'The next day, he went to investigate the truth,' showing his determination to uncover the mystery.
  • 踌躇之间，了无所见 - Means 'In his hesitation, he saw nothing,' indicating a moment of uncertainty.
  • 兴阑体倦 - Means 'His interest waned and he felt tired,' suggesting a decline in enthusiasm.
  • 方欲言还 - Means 'He was about to say he would return,' indicating a decision to leave.
  • 忽奇香馥郁，缥缈而来 - Means 'Suddenly, a strange fragrance came, faint and elusive,' describing a mysterious and enchanting aroma.
  • 韶异之，延仁以俟 - Means 'Shao found it strange and invited someone to wait,' indicating his curiosity and action.
  • 茶顷 - Means 'After a short while,' indicating a brief period of time.
  • 宫妆艳饰 - Means 'dressed in imperial makeup and adorned with beauty,' describing the appearance of the woman.
  • 貌类天仙 - Means 'Her appearance resembled a heavenly fairy,' emphasizing her beauty.
  • 小姬前导 - Means 'two young maids led the way,' indicating her entourage.
  • 一持黄金吊炉，一抱紫罗绣褥 - Means 'one held a golden incense burner, and one carried a purple embroidered carpet,' describing the items carried by the maids.
  • 冉冉登阶 - Means 'She ascended the steps gracefully,' indicating her elegant movement.
  • 意必贵家宅眷，临赏于此 - Means 'He thought she must be a noble lady visiting here,' suggesting his initial assumption.
  • 隐壁后避之 - Means 'He hid behind the wall to avoid her,' indicating his cautious behavior.
  • 小姬铺褥庭心，丽人席地而坐 - Means 'The young maids spread the carpet in the courtyard, and the beautiful woman sat on the ground,' describing the scene.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 京城女银瓶铁骑之韵 - Refers to the famous courtesan named 'Silver Bottle' and the sound of iron cavalry, which are cultural references from the Tang Dynasty.
  • 琵琶亭 - A famous pavilion in Jiujiang, often associated with Bai Juyi's 'Pipa Xing' (Song of the Pipa Player), a classic poem about a courtesan who plays the pipa.

  • 叹江山似旧，英雄尘土 - Poetic expression lamenting the unchanged landscape while heroes have turned to dust, reflecting on the impermanence of human glory.
  • 石马铜驼荆棘里 - Vivid image of stone horses and bronze camels (symbols of power and civilization) now overgrown with thorns, symbolizing the decay of former greatness.
  • 阅遍几番寒暑 - Means 'having witnessed many cycles of cold and heat,' indicating the passage of time.
